mn23:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn23:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Kumārakassapa was dwelling in Andhavana.
mn23:1.4: Then, late in the night, a certain *devatā*, beautiful in appearance, lighting up the whole of Andhavana, approached Venerable Kumārakassapa. Having approached, she stood to one side. Standing to one side, that *devatā* said this to Venerable Kumārakassapa:
mn23:2.1: “*bhikkhu*, *bhikkhu*, this anthill smokes by night, flames by day.
mn23:2.13: dig, wise one, taking the spade.’
mn23:2.14: Digging, the wise one, taking the spade, saw a fork in the road.
mn23:2.17: ‘Lift out the fork in the road;
mn23:2.18: dig, wise one, taking the spade.’
mn23:2.19: Digging, the wise one, taking the spade, saw a barrier.
mn23:2.23: dig, wise one, taking the spade.’
mn23:2.24: Digging, the wise one, taking the spade, saw a tortoise.
mn23:2.28: dig, wise one, taking the spade.’
mn23:2.29: Digging, the wise one, taking the spade, saw a chopping block.
mn23:2.33: dig, wise one, taking the spade.’
mn23:2.34: Digging, the wise one, taking the spade, saw a piece of meat.
mn23:2.38: dig, wise one, taking the spade.’
mn23:2.39: Digging, the wise one, taking the spade, saw a nāga.
mn23:2.4: Digging, the wise one, taking the spade, saw a bolt.
mn23:2.42: ‘Let the nāga remain; do not disturb the nāga; pay homage to the nāga.’
mn23:2.43: You should approach the *Bhagavā* and ask him these questions, *bhikkhu*; and as the *Bhagavā* answers you, so should you remember it.
mn23:2.44: I do not see anyone in this world with its *devatās*, Māra, and Brahmā, in this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its *devatās* and humans, who could satisfy your mind [*cittaṁ*] with an explanation of these questions, apart from the Tathāgata, or a disciple of the Tathāgata, or one who had heard it from them.”
mn23:2.46: Having said this, she vanished right there.
mn23:2.8: dig, wise one, taking the spade.’
mn23:2.9: Digging, the wise one, taking the spade, saw a swollen thing.
mn23:3.1: Then, when that night had passed, Venerable Kumārakassapa approached the *Bhagavā*. Having approached, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Venerable Kumārakassapa said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn23:3.10: What, bhante, is the anthill, what is smoking by night, what is flaming by day, who is the brahmin, who is the wise one, what is the spade, what is digging, what is the bolt, what is the swollen thing, what is the fork in the road, what is the barrier, what is the tortoise, what is the chopping block, what is the piece of meat, and what is the nāga?”
mn23:3.2: “Bhante, tonight a certain *devatā*, beautiful in appearance, lighting up the whole of Andhavana, approached me. Having approached, she stood to one side. Standing to one side, bhante, that *devatā* said this to me:
mn23:3.3: ‘*bhikkhu*, *bhikkhu*, this anthill smokes by night, flames by day.
mn23:3.6: Digging, the wise one, taking the spade …
mn23:3.7: or one who had heard it from them.’
mn23:3.8: That is what the *devatā* said, bhante.
mn23:3.9: Having said this, she vanished right there.
mn23:4.1: “‘Anthill,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for this body made of the four great elements, produced by mother and father, built up from rice and porridge, liable to constant cleaning and rubbing, to breaking up and destruction.
mn23:4.11: Lift out the bolt, abandon blindness [*avijjaṁ*];
mn23:4.12: dig, wise one, taking the spade: this is the meaning of that.
mn23:4.13: ‘Swollen thing,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for anger and distress.
mn23:4.14: Lift out the swollen thing, abandon anger and distress;
mn23:4.15: dig, wise one, taking the spade: this is the meaning of that.
mn23:4.16: ‘Fork in the road,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for doubt.
mn23:4.17: Lift out the fork in the road, abandon doubt;
mn23:4.18: dig, wise one, taking the spade: this is the meaning of that.
mn23:4.19: ‘Barrier,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for the five hindrances, that is:
mn23:4.2: Whatever, *bhikkhu*, one by day, concerning one’s activities, thinks over [*anuvitakketi*] and examines [*anuvicāreti*] by night—
mn23:4.20: the hindrance of sensual desire, the hindrance of ill will, the hindrance of dullness and drowsiness, the hindrance of restlessness and चिंता, the hindrance of doubt.
mn23:4.21: Lift out the barrier, abandon the five hindrances;
mn23:4.22: dig, wise one, taking the spade: this is the meaning of that.
mn23:4.23: ‘Tortoise,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for the five aggregates of clinging/feeding [*upādānakkhandhānaṁ*], that is:
mn23:4.24: the form aggregate of clinging/feeding [*rūpupādānakkhandhassa*], the *vedanā* aggregate of clinging/feeding [*vedanupādānakkhandhassa*], the *saññā* aggregate of clinging/feeding [*saññupādānakkhandhassa*], the *saṅkhāra* aggregate of clinging/feeding [*saṅkhārupādānakkhandhassa*], the consciousness [*viññāṇa*] aggregate of clinging/feeding [*viññāṇupādānakkhandhassa*].
mn23:4.25: Lift out the tortoise, abandon the five aggregates of clinging/feeding [*pañcupādānakkhandhe*];
mn23:4.26: dig, wise one, taking the spade: this is the meaning of that.
mn23:4.27: ‘Chopping block,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for the five cords of sensual pleasure—
mn23:4.28: forms cognizable by the eye, desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensuality, enticing,
mn23:4.32: tangible things cognizable by the body, desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensuality, enticing.
mn23:4.33: Lift out the chopping block, abandon the five cords of sensual pleasure;
mn23:4.34: dig, wise one, taking the spade: this is the meaning of that.
mn23:4.35: ‘Piece of meat,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for delight and lust.
mn23:4.36: Lift out the piece of meat, abandon delight and lust;
mn23:4.37: dig, wise one, taking the spade: this is the meaning of that.
mn23:4.38: ‘Nāga,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for a *bhikkhu* with destroyed *āsavas* [*khīṇāsavassa*].
mn23:4.39: Let the nāga remain; do not disturb the nāga; pay homage to the nāga: this is the meaning of that.”
mn23:4.4: Whatever, *bhikkhu*, having thought over [*anuvitakketvā*] and examined [*anuvicāretvā*] by night, one by day sets in motion in one’s activities by body, speech, and mind—
mn23:4.41: Satisfied, Venerable Kumārakassapa rejoiced in what the *Bhagavā* had said.
mn23:4.6: ‘Brahmin,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for the Tathāgata, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.
mn23:4.7: ‘Wise one,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for a trainee *bhikkhu*.
mn23:4.8: ‘Spade,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for noble discernment [*paññāya*].
mn23:4.9: ‘Digging,’ *bhikkhu*, is a designation for the arousing of energy.
